howItWorks.intro: This crypto dca calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Average Cost = Total Invested / Total Crypto Purchased
howItWorks.outro: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

faq.question: What is DCA?
faq.answer: Dollar-cost averaging means investing a fixed amount at regular intervals regardless of price. This smooths out volatility and avoids the risk of investing a lump sum at a market peak.

faq.question: Is DCA better than lump sum?
faq.answer: In trending markets, lump sum investing typically outperforms DCA about 2/3 of the time. However, DCA reduces psychological stress and downside risk during volatile periods.

faq.question: Is the Crypto DCA Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What weekly investment should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate weekly investment value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Crypto DCA Calculator?
sections.body: The Crypto DCA Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your weekly investment, number of weeks, starting price, you get instant results including total invested, crypto accumulated, avg cost basis. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total invested right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your weekly investment and need to find the right total invested. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weekly Investment ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your weekly investment — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of weeks, starting price, ending price. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total invested, crypto accumulated, avg cost basis. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your weekly investment before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total invested and crypto accumulated — they work together to give you the full picture.
